purpose: Backs the in-app Swarm Builder modal with CRUD operations on a dashboard's initialization.json: createSwarm (also resets logs.json + clears progress/), addTask, updateTask, removeTask (with depends_on cleanup + progress file deletion), addWave, removeWave (with cascading task delete), nextTaskId (auto-numbering), and validateDependencies (checks for missing references + cycles via Kahn's algorithm). Wave totals are recalculated after every task mutation, and waves with zero tasks are auto-removed.

exports.name: addTask
exports.signature: (dashboardId, {id, title, wave, layer?, directory?, depends_on?, description?}) => init

gotchas: createSwarm RESETS the dashboard: it overwrites initialization.json, replaces logs.json with a single 'Swarm created' entry, and unlinks every JSON file in the progress/ subdir. There is no confirmation guard — any caller using this on a live dashboard will wipe its history.

addTask's `description` field is added to the agent object but is NOT part of the standard initialization.json schema (it lives outside agents[].id/title/wave/layer/directory/depends_on). Some downstream consumers may not expect it.
removeTask deletes the task's progress JSON file on disk — re-adding the task with the same ID produces a clean slate. There is no undo.
removeWave cascades to delete every task in that wave AND scrubs depends_on arrays — a deeply downstream chain can be unintentionally orphaned.

nextTaskId assumes the convention W.N (wave.subtask). IDs like '2.1r' (replanner repair tasks from SwarmOrchestrator.applyReplan) parse fine but won't be accounted for when computing maxSub if the suffix breaks parseInt.
validateDependencies uses Kahn's algorithm on a freshly-built indegree map — if multiple agents share the same id (data corruption), the cycle-check undercounts visited.
DEFAULT_LOGS is imported but never used — dead import.
patterns: Read-modify-write pattern: every public function reads init, mutates it, calls writeInit().
Falls back to DEFAULT_INITIALIZATION (deep-cloned via JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(...))) if the dashboard has no init file yet.
recalcWaves enforces invariants: wave.total reflects current agents, empty waves are pruned, init.task.total_tasks/total_waves are updated.
conventions: Task IDs are strings shaped 'W.N' (wave.subtask) — must be unique across the dashboard.
All mutations return the full updated init object so callers can immediately re-render without a separate read.
